title: Discussion Paper: Driving Effective Carbon Markets in Canada
content:
As announced in the Climate Competitiveness Strategy, Canada is engaging provinces and territories to improve Canada's industrial carbon pricing systems. As part of that initiative, this paper seeks feedback on the federal â€˜benchmark', which sets the minimum national stringency standards that all provincial and territorial systems must meet while allowing flexibility in design to address jurisdictional circumstances.
The federal government is working to update the benchmark to ensure that industrial pricing provides the necessary incentives and framework to drive decarbonization, clean technology investment and competitiveness over the short- and long-term. The proposals and questions in this paper are intended to seek constructive engagement on strengthening and improving the benchmark while maintaining flexibility for provincial and territorial system design.
author: Analysts Account
